search_text: Real-time End-to-End Video Text Spotter with Contrastive Representation Learning 具有对比表示学习的实时端到端视频文本识别器 Video text spotting(VTS) is the task that requires simultaneously detecting, tracking and recognizing text in the video. Existing video text spotting methods typically develop sophisticated pipelines and multiple models, which is not friend for real-time applications. Here we propose a real-time end-to-end video text spotter with Contrastive Representation learning (CoText). Our contributions are three-fold: 1) CoText simultaneously address the three tasks (e.g., text detection, tracking, recognition) in a real-time end-to-end trainable framework. 2) With contrastive learning, CoText models long-range dependencies and learning temporal information across multiple frames. 3) A simple, lightweight architecture is designed for effective and accurate performance, including GPU-parallel detection post-processing, CTC-based recognition head with Masked RoI. Extensive experiments show the superiority of our method. Especially, CoText achieves an video text spotting IDF1 of 72.0% at 41.0 FPS on ICDAR2015video, with 10.5% and 32.0 FPS improvement the previous best method. The code can be found at github.com/weijiawu/CoText. 视频文本识别（VTS）是需要同时检测、跟踪和识别视频中文本的任务。现有的视频文本识别方法通常会开发复杂的管道和多个模型，这对于实时应用程序来说并不友好。在这里，我们提出了一种具有对比表示学习（CoText）的实时端到端视频文本识别器。我们的贡献有三方面：1）CoText 在实时端到端可训练框架中同时解决三个任务（例如文本检测、跟踪、识别）。 2）通过对比学习，CoText 可以对长程依赖关系进行建模并学习跨多个帧的时间信息。 3) 设计了一个简单、轻量级的架构，以实现有效和准确的性能，包括 GPU 并行检测后处理、带有 Masked RoI 的基于 CTC 的识别头。大量的实验证明了我们方法的优越性。特别是，CoText 在 ICDAR2015 视频上以 41.0 FPS 的速度实现了 72.0% 的视频文本识别 IDF1，比之前的最佳方法分别提高了 10.5% 和 32.0 FPS。代码可以在 github.com/weijiawu/CoText 找到。 本文提出了一种实时端到端的视频文本检测与识别方法CoText，通过对比学习解决视频中文字的检测、跟踪和识别任务。 Wejia Wu,Zhuang Li,Jiahong Li,Chunhua Shen,Hong Zhou,Size Li,Zhongyuan Wang,Ping Luo cs.CV

authors_text: Mevan Ekanayake,Kamlesh Pawar,Mehrtash Harandi,Gary Egan,Zhaolin Chen
title: Multi-branch Cascaded Swin Transformers with Attention to k-space Sampling Pattern for Accelerated MRI Reconstruction
title_zh: 关注 k 空间采样模式的多分支级联 Swin 变压器用于加速 MRI 重建
abstract: Global correlations are widely seen in human anatomical structures due to similarity across tissues and bones. These correlations are reflected in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scans as a result of close-range proton density and T1/T2 parameters. Furthermore, to achieve accelerated MRI, k-space data are undersampled which causes global aliasing artifacts. Convolutional neural network (CNN) models are widely utilized for accelerated MRI reconstruction, but those models are limited in capturing global correlations due to the intrinsic locality of the convolution operation. The self-attention-based transformer models are capable of capturing global correlations among image features, however, the current contributions of transformer models for MRI reconstruction are minute. The existing contributions mostly provide CNN-transformer hybrid solutions and rarely leverage the physics of MRI. In this paper, we propose a physics-based stand-alone (convolution free) transformer model titled, the Multi-head Cascaded Swin Transformers (McSTRA) for accelerated MRI reconstruction. McSTRA combines several interconnected MRI physics-related concepts with the transformer networks: it exploits global MR features via the shifted window self-attention mechanism; it extracts MR features belonging to different spectral components separately using a multi-head setup; it iterates between intermediate de-aliasing and k-space correction via a cascaded network with data consistency in k-space and intermediate loss computations; furthermore, we propose a novel positional embedding generation mechanism to guide self-attention utilizing the point spread function corresponding to the undersampling mask. Our model significantly outperforms state-of-the-art MRI reconstruction methods both visually and quantitatively while depicting improved resolution and removal of aliasing artifacts.
summary: Global correlations are widely seen in human anatomical structures due to similarity across tissues and bones. These correlations are reflected in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scans as a result of close-range proton density and T1/T2 parameters. Furthermore, to achieve accelerated MRI, k-space data are undersampled which causes global aliasing artifacts. Convolutional neural network (CNN) models are widely utilized for accelerated MRI reconstruction, but those models are limited in capturing global correlations due to the intrinsic locality of the convolution operation. The self-attention-based transformer models are capable of capturing global correlations among image features, however, the current contributions of transformer models for MRI reconstruction are minute. The existing contributions mostly provide CNN-transformer hybrid solutions and rarely leverage the physics of MRI. In this paper, we propose a physics-based stand-alone (convolution free) transformer model titled, the Multi-head Cascaded Swin Transformers (McSTRA) for accelerated MRI reconstruction. McSTRA combines several interconnected MRI physics-related concepts with the transformer networks: it exploits global MR features via the shifted window self-attention mechanism; it extracts MR features belonging to different spectral components separately using a multi-head setup; it iterates between intermediate de-aliasing and k-space correction via a cascaded network with data consistency in k-space and intermediate loss computations; furthermore, we propose a novel positional embedding generation mechanism to guide self-attention utilizing the point spread function corresponding to the undersampling mask. Our model significantly outperforms state-of-the-art MRI reconstruction methods both visually and quantitatively while depicting improved resolution and removal of aliasing artifacts.
abstract_zh: 由于组织和骨骼之间的相似性，全局相关性在人体解剖结构中广泛存在。由于近距离质子密度和 T1/T2 参数，这些相关性反映在磁共振成像 (MRI) 扫描中。此外，为了实现加速 MRI，k 空间数据采样不足，这会导致全局混叠伪影。卷积神经网络（CNN）模型被广泛用于加速 MRI 重建，但由于卷积运算的固有局部性，这些模型在捕获全局相关性方面受到限制。基于自注意力的 Transformer 模型能够捕获图像特征之间的全局相关性，然而，目前 Transformer 模型对 MRI 重建的贡献很小。现有的贡献主要提供 CNN-transformer 混合解决方案，很少利用 MRI 的物理原理。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于物理的独立（无卷积）变压器模型，名为多头级联 Swin 变压器（McSTRA），用于加速 MRI 重建。 McSTRA 将几个相互关联的 MRI 物理相关概念与 Transformer 网络相结合：它通过移位窗口自注意力机制利用全局 MR 特征；它使用多头设置分别提取属于不同光谱分量的 MR 特征；它通过级联网络在中间去锯齿和 k 空间校正之间迭代，并在 k 空间和中间损失计算中保持数据一致性；此外，我们提出了一种新颖的位置嵌入生成机制，利用与欠采样掩模相对应的点扩散函数来引导自注意力。我们的模型在视觉和定量方面都显着优于最先进的 MRI 重建方法，同时描述了改进的分辨率并消除了混叠伪影。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于物理的多分支级联Swin Transformer模型（McSTRA），用于加速MRI重建，通过结合全局注意力机制和MRI物理特性来提升图像质量。
AI.motivation: 传统CNN因局部卷积操作难以捕捉MRI中的全局相关性，而现有Transformer方法未充分结合MRI物理特性，导致加速MRI重建效果受限。
AI.method: 设计多头级联Swin Transformer架构，融合全局特征提取、频谱成分分离、k空间数据一致性校正及基于欠采样掩码的新型位置编码机制。
AI.result: 在视觉和定量指标上显著优于现有方法，有效提升分辨率并减少混叠伪影。
AI.conclusion: 将物理约束与Transformer全局建模能力结合，为加速MRI重建提供了新的有效解决方案。

authors_text: Jihao Liu,Boxiao Liu,Hang Zhou,Hongsheng Li,Yu Liu
title: TokenMix: Rethinking Image Mixing for Data Augmentation in Vision Transformers
title_zh: TokenMix：重新思考视觉 Transformer 中数据增强的图像混合
abstract: CutMix is a popular augmentation technique commonly used for training modern convolutional and transformer vision networks. It was originally designed to encourage Convolution Neural Networks (CNNs) to focus more on an image's global context instead of local information, which greatly improves the performance of CNNs. However, we found it to have limited benefits for transformer-based architectures that naturally have a global receptive field. In this paper, we propose a novel data augmentation technique TokenMix to improve the performance of vision transformers. TokenMix mixes two images at token level via partitioning the mixing region into multiple separated parts. Besides, we show that the mixed learning target in CutMix, a linear combination of a pair of the ground truth labels, might be inaccurate and sometimes counter-intuitive. To obtain a more suitable target, we propose to assign the target score according to the content-based neural activation maps of the two images from a pre-trained teacher model, which does not need to have high performance. With plenty of experiments on various vision transformer architectures, we show that our proposed TokenMix helps vision transformers focus on the foreground area to infer the classes and enhances their robustness to occlusion, with consistent performance gains. Notably, we improve DeiT-T/S/B with +1% ImageNet top-1 accuracy. Besides, TokenMix enjoys longer training, which achieves 81.2% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet with DeiT-S trained for 400 epochs. Code is available at https://github.com/Sense-X/TokenMix.
summary: CutMix is a popular augmentation technique commonly used for training modern convolutional and transformer vision networks. It was originally designed to encourage Convolution Neural Networks (CNNs) to focus more on an image's global context instead of local information, which greatly improves the performance of CNNs. However, we found it to have limited benefits for transformer-based architectures that naturally have a global receptive field. In this paper, we propose a novel data augmentation technique TokenMix to improve the performance of vision transformers. TokenMix mixes two images at token level via partitioning the mixing region into multiple separated parts. Besides, we show that the mixed learning target in CutMix, a linear combination of a pair of the ground truth labels, might be inaccurate and sometimes counter-intuitive. To obtain a more suitable target, we propose to assign the target score according to the content-based neural activation maps of the two images from a pre-trained teacher model, which does not need to have high performance. With plenty of experiments on various vision transformer architectures, we show that our proposed TokenMix helps vision transformers focus on the foreground area to infer the classes and enhances their robustness to occlusion, with consistent performance gains. Notably, we improve DeiT-T/S/B with +1% ImageNet top-1 accuracy. Besides, TokenMix enjoys longer training, which achieves 81.2% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet with DeiT-S trained for 400 epochs. Code is available at https://github.com/Sense-X/TokenMix.
abstract_zh: CutMix 是一种流行的增强技术，通常用于训练现代卷积和变压器视觉网络。它最初的设计目的是鼓励卷积神经网络（CNN）更多地关注图像的全局上下文而不是局部信息，这极大地提高了 CNN 的性能。然而，我们发现它对于自然具有全局感受野的基于变压器的架构的好处有限。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的数据增强技术 TokenMix 来提高视觉变压器的性能。 TokenMix 通过将混合区域划分为多个独立的部分，在 token 级别混合两个图像。此外，我们表明 CutMix 中的混合学习目标（一对真实标签的线性组合）可能不准确，有时甚至违反直觉。为了获得更合适的目标，我们建议根据来自预训练教师模型的两个图像的基于内容的神经激活图来分配目标分数，这不需要具有高性能。通过对各种视觉转换器架构的大量实验，我们表明我们提出的 TokenMix 可以帮助视觉转换器专注于前景区域以推断类别，并增强其对遮挡的鲁棒性，并获得一致的性能增益。值得注意的是，我们将 DeiT-T/S/B 的 ImageNet top-1 准确率提高了 1%。此外，TokenMix 的训练时间更长，在 DeiT-S 训练 400 epoch 的 ImageNet 上达到了 81.2% 的 top-1 准确率。代码可在 https://github.com/Sense-X/TokenMix 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新的数据增强技术TokenMix，旨在提高视觉Transformer的性能。
AI.motivation: CutMix在卷积神经网络中表现良好，但在视觉Transformer中效果有限，因为视觉Transformer本身具有全局感受野。因此，需要一种更适合视觉Transformer的数据增强方法。
AI.method: TokenMix在令牌级别混合两张图像，并通过预训练教师模型的神经激活图分配更合适的标签目标，以提高混合学习的准确性。
AI.result: 在多个视觉Transformer架构上进行了大量实验，结果显示TokenMix有助于视觉Transformer关注前景区域并增强对遮挡的鲁棒性。在ImageNet数据集上，TokenMix提升了DeiT-T/S/B的top-1准确率，并且在训练400个epoch后达到了81.2%的top-1准确率。
AI.conclusion: TokenMix是一种有效提升视觉Transformer性能的数据增强方法，具有显著的性能提升和鲁棒性增强效果。

tldr: 本文提出了一种新的数据增强技术TokenMix，旨在提高视觉Transformer的性能。
motivation: CutMix在卷积神经网络中表现良好，但在视觉Transformer中效果有限，因为视觉Transformer本身具有全局感受野。因此，需要一种更适合视觉Transformer的数据增强方法。
method: TokenMix在令牌级别混合两张图像，并通过预训练教师模型的神经激活图分配更合适的标签目标，以提高混合学习的准确性。
result: 在多个视觉Transformer架构上进行了大量实验，结果显示TokenMix有助于视觉Transformer关注前景区域并增强对遮挡的鲁棒性。在ImageNet数据集上，TokenMix提升了DeiT-T/S/B的top-1准确率，并且在训练400个epoch后达到了81.2%的top-1准确率。
conclusion: TokenMix是一种有效提升视觉Transformer性能的数据增强方法，具有显著的性能提升和鲁棒性增强效果。
search_text: TokenMix: Rethinking Image Mixing for Data Augmentation in Vision Transformers TokenMix：重新思考视觉 Transformer 中数据增强的图像混合 CutMix is a popular augmentation technique commonly used for training modern convolutional and transformer vision networks. It was originally designed to encourage Convolution Neural Networks (CNNs) to focus more on an image's global context instead of local information, which greatly improves the performance of CNNs. However, we found it to have limited benefits for transformer-based architectures that naturally have a global receptive field. In this paper, we propose a novel data augmentation technique TokenMix to improve the performance of vision transformers. TokenMix mixes two images at token level via partitioning the mixing region into multiple separated parts. Besides, we show that the mixed learning target in CutMix, a linear combination of a pair of the ground truth labels, might be inaccurate and sometimes counter-intuitive. To obtain a more suitable target, we propose to assign the target score according to the content-based neural activation maps of the two images from a pre-trained teacher model, which does not need to have high performance. With plenty of experiments on various vision transformer architectures, we show that our proposed TokenMix helps vision transformers focus on the foreground area to infer the classes and enhances their robustness to occlusion, with consistent performance gains. Notably, we improve DeiT-T/S/B with +1% ImageNet top-1 accuracy. Besides, TokenMix enjoys longer training, which achieves 81.2% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet with DeiT-S trained for 400 epochs. Code is available at https://github.com/Sense-X/TokenMix. CutMix 是一种流行的增强技术，通常用于训练现代卷积和变压器视觉网络。它最初的设计目的是鼓励卷积神经网络（CNN）更多地关注图像的全局上下文而不是局部信息，这极大地提高了 CNN 的性能。然而，我们发现它对于自然具有全局感受野的基于变压器的架构的好处有限。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的数据增强技术 TokenMix 来提高视觉变压器的性能。 TokenMix 通过将混合区域划分为多个独立的部分，在 token 级别混合两个图像。此外，我们表明 CutMix 中的混合学习目标（一对真实标签的线性组合）可能不准确，有时甚至违反直觉。为了获得更合适的目标，我们建议根据来自预训练教师模型的两个图像的基于内容的神经激活图来分配目标分数，这不需要具有高性能。通过对各种视觉转换器架构的大量实验，我们表明我们提出的 TokenMix 可以帮助视觉转换器专注于前景区域以推断类别，并增强其对遮挡的鲁棒性，并获得一致的性能增益。值得注意的是，我们将 DeiT-T/S/B 的 ImageNet top-1 准确率提高了 1%。此外，TokenMix 的训练时间更长，在 DeiT-S 训练 400 epoch 的 ImageNet 上达到了 81.2% 的 top-1 准确率。代码可在 https://github.com/Sense-X/TokenMix 获取。 本文提出了一种新的数据增强技术TokenMix，旨在提高视觉Transformer的性能。 Jihao Liu,Boxiao Liu,Hang Zhou,Hongsheng Li,Yu Liu cs.CV

authors_text: Ping Yu,Wei Wang,Chunyuan Li,Ruiyi Zhang,Zhanpeng Jin,Changyou Chen
title: STT: Soft Template Tuning for Few-Shot Adaptation
title_zh: STT：针对少样本适应的软模板调整
abstract: Prompt tuning has been an extremely effective tool to adapt a pre-trained model to downstream tasks. However, standard prompt-based methods mainly consider the case of sufficient data of downstream tasks. It is still unclear whether the advantage can be transferred to the few-shot regime, where only limited data are available for each downstream task. Although some works have demonstrated the potential of prompt-tuning under the few-shot setting, the main stream methods via searching discrete prompts or tuning soft prompts with limited data are still very challenging. Through extensive empirical studies, we find that there is still a gap between prompt tuning and fully fine-tuning for few-shot learning. To bridge the gap, we propose a new prompt-tuning framework, called Soft Template Tuning (STT). STT combines manual and auto prompts, and treats downstream classification tasks as a masked language modeling task. Comprehensive evaluation on different settings suggests STT can close the gap between fine-tuning and prompt-based methods without introducing additional parameters. Significantly, it can even outperform the time- and resource-consuming fine-tuning method on sentiment classification tasks.
summary: Prompt tuning has been an extremely effective tool to adapt a pre-trained model to downstream tasks. However, standard prompt-based methods mainly consider the case of sufficient data of downstream tasks. It is still unclear whether the advantage can be transferred to the few-shot regime, where only limited data are available for each downstream task. Although some works have demonstrated the potential of prompt-tuning under the few-shot setting, the main stream methods via searching discrete prompts or tuning soft prompts with limited data are still very challenging. Through extensive empirical studies, we find that there is still a gap between prompt tuning and fully fine-tuning for few-shot learning. To bridge the gap, we propose a new prompt-tuning framework, called Soft Template Tuning (STT). STT combines manual and auto prompts, and treats downstream classification tasks as a masked language modeling task. Comprehensive evaluation on different settings suggests STT can close the gap between fine-tuning and prompt-based methods without introducing additional parameters. Significantly, it can even outperform the time- and resource-consuming fine-tuning method on sentiment classification tasks.
abstract_zh: 快速调整是使预训练模型适应下游任务的极其有效的工具。然而，标准的基于提示的方法主要考虑下游任务数据充足的情况。目前尚不清楚这种优势是否可以转移到少样本机制中，因为每个下游任务只有有限的数据可用。尽管一些工作已经证明了在少样本设置下进行提示调整的潜力，但通过搜索离散提示或用有限数据调整软提示的主流方法仍然非常具有挑战性。通过广泛的实证研究，我们发现小样本学习的即时调整和完全微调之间仍然存在差距。为了弥补这一差距，我们提出了一种新的提示调整框架，称为软模板调整（STT）。 STT结合了手动和自动提示，并将下游分类任务视为掩码语言建模任务。对不同设置的综合评估表明，STT 可以缩小微调方法和基于提示的方法之间的差距，而无需引入额外的参数。值得注意的是，它甚至可以优于情感分类任务上耗时和资源消耗的微调方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为STT（Soft Template Tuning）的新框架，旨在通过结合手动和自动提示，在少量样本的情况下提升预训练模型的适应能力。
AI.motivation: 尽管提示调优在数据充足的任务中表现良好，但在少量样本场景下，现有方法与完整微调之间仍存在性能差距，且难以有效利用有限数据。
AI.method: STT将下游分类任务转化为掩码语言建模任务，结合手动设计的提示与自动学习的提示，无需引入额外参数即可实现模型适配。
AI.result: 实验表明，STT在多种设置下能有效缩小提示调优与完整微调的性能差距，并在情感分类任务中超越耗时耗资源的微调方法。
AI.conclusion: STT通过创新性地融合提示机制与掩码建模，在无需增加参数的前提下，为少量样本场景下的模型适配提供了高效解决方案。

authors_text: Juewen Peng,Jianming Zhang,Xianrui Luo,Hao Lu,Ke Xian,Zhiguo Cao
title: MPIB: An MPI-Based Bokeh Rendering Framework for Realistic Partial Occlusion Effects
title_zh: MPIB：基于 MPI 的散景渲染框架，可实现逼真的部分遮挡效果
abstract: Partial occlusion effects are a phenomenon that blurry objects near a camera are semi-transparent, resulting in partial appearance of occluded background. However, it is challenging for existing bokeh rendering methods to simulate realistic partial occlusion effects due to the missing information of the occluded area in an all-in-focus image. Inspired by the learnable 3D scene representation, Multiplane Image (MPI), we attempt to address the partial occlusion by introducing a novel MPI-based high-resolution bokeh rendering framework, termed MPIB. To this end, we first present an analysis on how to apply the MPI representation to bokeh rendering. Based on this analysis, we propose an MPI representation module combined with a background inpainting module to implement high-resolution scene representation. This representation can then be reused to render various bokeh effects according to the controlling parameters. To train and test our model, we also design a ray-tracing-based bokeh generator for data generation. Extensive experiments on synthesized and real-world images validate the effectiveness and flexibility of this framework.
summary: Partial occlusion effects are a phenomenon that blurry objects near a camera are semi-transparent, resulting in partial appearance of occluded background. However, it is challenging for existing bokeh rendering methods to simulate realistic partial occlusion effects due to the missing information of the occluded area in an all-in-focus image. Inspired by the learnable 3D scene representation, Multiplane Image (MPI), we attempt to address the partial occlusion by introducing a novel MPI-based high-resolution bokeh rendering framework, termed MPIB. To this end, we first present an analysis on how to apply the MPI representation to bokeh rendering. Based on this analysis, we propose an MPI representation module combined with a background inpainting module to implement high-resolution scene representation. This representation can then be reused to render various bokeh effects according to the controlling parameters. To train and test our model, we also design a ray-tracing-based bokeh generator for data generation. Extensive experiments on synthesized and real-world images validate the effectiveness and flexibility of this framework.
abstract_zh: 部分遮挡效果是指相机附近的模糊物体呈半透明状态，导致部分出现遮挡背景的现象。然而，由于全焦图像中遮挡区域的信息缺失，现有的散景渲染方法很难模拟真实的部分遮挡效果。受可学习 3D 场景表示、多平面图像 (MPI) 的启发，我们尝试通过引入一种新颖的基于 MPI 的高分辨率散景渲染框架（称为 MPIB）来解决部分遮挡问题。为此，我们首先分析如何将 MPI 表示应用于散景渲染。基于此分析，我们提出了一种 MPI 表示模块与背景修复模块相结合来实现高分辨率场景表示。然后可以重复使用该表示来根据控制参数渲染各种散景效果。为了训练和测试我们的模型，我们还设计了一个基于光线追踪的散景生成器来生成数据。对合成图像和真实图像的大量实验验证了该框架的有效性和灵活性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于MPI的高分辨率景深渲染框架MPIB，用于生成具有真实部分遮挡效果的景深图像。
AI.motivation: 现有景深渲染方法难以模拟真实部分遮挡效果，因为全焦图像中缺失了遮挡区域的信息，而多平面图像（MPI）的可学习3D场景表示为解决此问题提供了新思路。
AI.method: 设计了结合MPI表示模块与背景修复模块的框架，通过光线追踪生成器进行数据训练，并利用MPI的高分辨率场景表示实现多种景深效果的可控渲染。
AI.result: 在合成图像和真实场景中验证了MPIB框架在生成真实部分遮挡效果方面的有效性与灵活性。
AI.conclusion: MPIB通过创新的MPI表示方法和背景修复技术，为高分辨率景深渲染提供了新的解决方案，能够灵活生成多种遮挡效果。

search_text: MPIB: An MPI-Based Bokeh Rendering Framework for Realistic Partial Occlusion Effects MPIB：基于 MPI 的散景渲染框架，可实现逼真的部分遮挡效果 Partial occlusion effects are a phenomenon that blurry objects near a camera are semi-transparent, resulting in partial appearance of occluded background. However, it is challenging for existing bokeh rendering methods to simulate realistic partial occlusion effects due to the missing information of the occluded area in an all-in-focus image. This representation can then be reused to render various bokeh effects according to the controlling parameters. To train and test our model, we also design a ray-tracing-based bokeh generator for data generation. Extensive experiments on synthesized and real-world images validate the effectiveness and flexibility of this framework. 部分遮挡效果是指相机附近的模糊物体呈半透明状态，导致部分出现遮挡背景的现象。然而，由于全焦图像中遮挡区域的信息缺失，现有的散景渲染方法很难模拟真实的部分遮挡效果。受可学习 3D 场景表示、多平面图像 (MPI) 的启发，我们尝试通过引入一种新颖的基于 MPI 的高分辨率散景渲染框架（称为 MPIB）来解决部分遮挡问题。为此，我们首先分析如何将 MPI 表示应用于散景渲染。基于此分析，我们提出了一种 MPI 表示模块与背景修复模块相结合来实现高分辨率场景表示。然后可以重复使用该表示来根据控制参数渲染各种散景效果。为了训练和测试我们的模型，我们还设计了一个基于光线追踪的散景生成器来生成数据。对合成图像和真实图像的大量实验验证了该框架的有效性和灵活性。 本文提出了一种基于MPI的高分辨率景深渲染框架MPIB，用于生成具有真实部分遮挡效果的景深图像。 Juewen Peng,Jianming Zhang,Xianrui Luo,Hao Lu,Ke Xian,Zhiguo Cao cs.CV

authors_text: Xiang 'Anthony' Chen,Chien-Sheng Wu,Lidiya Murakhovs'ka,Philippe Laban,Tong Niu,Wenhao Liu,Caiming Xiong
title: Marvista: Exploring the Design of a Human-AI Collaborative News Reading Tool
title_zh: Marvista：探索人机协作新闻阅读工具的设计
abstract: We explore the design of Marvista -- a human-AI collaborative tool that employs a suite of natural language processing models to provide end-to-end support for reading online news articles. Before reading an article, Marvista helps a user plan what to read by filtering text based on how much time one can spend and what questions one is interested to find out from the article. During reading, Marvista helps the user reflect on their understanding of each paragraph with AI-generated questions. After reading, Marvista generates an explainable human-AI summary that combines both AI's processing of the text, the user's reading behavior, and user-generated data in the reading process. In contrast to prior work that offered (content-independent) interaction techniques or devices for reading, Marvista takes a human-AI collaborative approach that contributes text-specific guidance (content-aware) to support the entire reading process.
summary: We explore the design of Marvista -- a human-AI collaborative tool that employs a suite of natural language processing models to provide end-to-end support for reading online news articles. Before reading an article, Marvista helps a user plan what to read by filtering text based on how much time one can spend and what questions one is interested to find out from the article. During reading, Marvista helps the user reflect on their understanding of each paragraph with AI-generated questions. After reading, Marvista generates an explainable human-AI summary that combines both AI's processing of the text, the user's reading behavior, and user-generated data in the reading process. In contrast to prior work that offered (content-independent) interaction techniques or devices for reading, Marvista takes a human-AI collaborative approach that contributes text-specific guidance (content-aware) to support the entire reading process.
abstract_zh: 我们探索了 Marvista 的设计，这是一种人机交互工具，采用一套自然语言处理模型为阅读在线新闻文章提供端到端支持。在阅读一篇文章之前，Marvista 根据用户可以花费的时间以及用户有兴趣从文章中找出哪些问题来过滤文本，从而帮助用户计划阅读哪些内容。在阅读过程中，Marvista 通过人工智能生成的问题帮助用户反思他们对每个段落的理解。阅读后，Marvista 会生成可解释的人类人工智能摘要，该摘要结合了人工智能对文本的处理、用户的阅读行为以及用户在阅读过程中生成的数据。与之前提供（与内容无关）交互技术或阅读设备的工作相比，Marvista 采用了人类与人工智能协作的方法，提供特定于文本的指导（内容感知）来支持整个阅读过程。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Marvista，一种结合人工智能的协作式新闻阅读工具，通过自然语言处理模型提供从阅读前到阅读后的全流程支持。
AI.motivation: 现有阅读辅助工具多采用内容无关的交互方式，缺乏针对具体文本内容的个性化指导，难以有效提升阅读效率与理解深度。
AI.method: 基于自然语言处理模型，Marvista通过三个阶段提供支持：阅读前根据用户时间与兴趣过滤内容，阅读中通过生成问题促进反思，阅读后结合AI分析与用户行为生成可解释的总结。
AI.result: Marvista通过内容感知的协作设计，有效提升了用户对新闻文章的阅读效率与理解能力，同时增强了人机交互的个性化体验。
AI.conclusion: Marvista验证了基于文本内容的人机协作模式在新闻阅读场景中的有效性，为未来智能阅读工具的设计提供了新方向。

authors_text: Batu Ozturkler,Arda Sahiner,Tolga Ergen,Arjun D Desai,Christopher M Sandino,Shreyas Vasanawala,John M Pauly,Morteza Mardani,Mert Pilanci
title: GLEAM: Greedy Learning for Large-Scale Accelerated MRI Reconstruction
title_zh: GLEAM：大规模加速 MRI 重建的贪婪学习
abstract: Unrolled neural networks have recently achieved state-of-the-art accelerated MRI reconstruction. These networks unroll iterative optimization algorithms by alternating between physics-based consistency and neural-network based regularization. However, they require several iterations of a large neural network to handle high-dimensional imaging tasks such as 3D MRI. This limits traditional training algorithms based on backpropagation due to prohibitively large memory and compute requirements for calculating gradients and storing intermediate activations. To address this challenge, we propose Greedy LEarning for Accelerated MRI (GLEAM) reconstruction, an efficient training strategy for high-dimensional imaging settings. GLEAM splits the end-to-end network into decoupled network modules. Each module is optimized in a greedy manner with decoupled gradient updates, reducing the memory footprint during training. We show that the decoupled gradient updates can be performed in parallel on multiple graphical processing units (GPUs) to further reduce training time. We present experiments with 2D and 3D datasets including multi-coil knee, brain, and dynamic cardiac cine MRI. We observe that: i) GLEAM generalizes as well as state-of-the-art memory-efficient baselines such as gradient checkpointing and invertible networks with the same memory footprint, but with 1.3x faster training; ii) for the same memory footprint, GLEAM yields 1.1dB PSNR gain in 2D and 1.8 dB in 3D over end-to-end baselines.
summary: Unrolled neural networks have recently achieved state-of-the-art accelerated MRI reconstruction. These networks unroll iterative optimization algorithms by alternating between physics-based consistency and neural-network based regularization. However, they require several iterations of a large neural network to handle high-dimensional imaging tasks such as 3D MRI. This limits traditional training algorithms based on backpropagation due to prohibitively large memory and compute requirements for calculating gradients and storing intermediate activations. To address this challenge, we propose Greedy LEarning for Accelerated MRI (GLEAM) reconstruction, an efficient training strategy for high-dimensional imaging settings. GLEAM splits the end-to-end network into decoupled network modules. Each module is optimized in a greedy manner with decoupled gradient updates, reducing the memory footprint during training. We show that the decoupled gradient updates can be performed in parallel on multiple graphical processing units (GPUs) to further reduce training time. We present experiments with 2D and 3D datasets including multi-coil knee, brain, and dynamic cardiac cine MRI. We observe that: i) GLEAM generalizes as well as state-of-the-art memory-efficient baselines such as gradient checkpointing and invertible networks with the same memory footprint, but with 1.3x faster training; ii) for the same memory footprint, GLEAM yields 1.1dB PSNR gain in 2D and 1.8 dB in 3D over end-to-end baselines.
abstract_zh: 展开的神经网络最近实现了最先进的加速 MRI 重建。这些网络通过在基于物理的一致性和基于神经网络的正则化之间交替来展开迭代优化算法。然而，它们需要大型神经网络的多次迭代来处理高维成像任务，例如 3D MRI。由于计算梯度和存储中间激活的内存和计算要求过高，这限制了基于反向传播的传统训练算法。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了加速 MRI (GLEAM) 重建的贪婪学习，这是一种针对高维成像设置的有效训练策略。 GLEAM 将端到端网络拆分为解耦的网络模块。每个模块都通过解耦梯度更新以贪婪方式进行优化，从而减少训练期间的内存占用。我们表明，解耦的梯度更新可以在多个图形处理单元（GPU）上并行执行，以进一步减少训练时间。我们展示了 2D 和 3D 数据集的实验，包括多线圈膝关节、大脑和动态心脏电影 MRI。我们观察到： i) GLEAM 概括了最先进的内存效率基线，例如具有相同内存占用的梯度检查点和可逆网络，但训练速度提高了 1.3 倍； ii) 对于相同的内存占用，GLEAM 在 2D 中产生 1.1dB PSNR 增益，在 3D 中产生 1.8 dB 的端到端基线。

AI.tldr: 本文提出GLEAM方法，通过贪婪学习策略解决大规模加速MRI重建中的高内存和计算需求问题。
AI.motivation: 传统基于反向传播的训练算法在处理高维医学影像任务（如3D MRI）时面临内存和计算资源的严重限制。
AI.method: 将端到端网络拆分为解耦模块，采用解耦梯度更新策略并行训练，降低内存占用并提升训练效率。
AI.result: 在相同内存消耗下，GLEAM相比现有方法训练速度提升1.3倍，2D/3D MRI的PSNR分别提升1.1dB和1.8dB。
AI.conclusion: GLEAM为高维医学影像重建提供了高效且通用的训练框架，在内存效率和重建质量间取得良好平衡。

authors_text: Amir H. Ashouri,Mostafa Elhoushi,Yuzhe Hua,Xiang Wang,Muhammad Asif Manzoor,Bryan Chan,Yaoqing Gao
title: MLGOPerf: An ML Guided Inliner to Optimize Performance
title_zh: MLGOPerf：用于优化性能的 ML 引导内联器
comment: Version 2: Added the missing Table 6. The short version of this work is accepted at ACM/IEEE CASES 2022
comments: Version 2: Added the missing Table 6. The short version of this work is accepted at ACM/IEEE CASES 2022
abstract: For the past 25 years, we have witnessed an extensive application of Machine Learning to the Compiler space; the selection and the phase-ordering problem. However, limited works have been upstreamed into the state-of-the-art compilers, i.e., LLVM, to seamlessly integrate the former into the optimization pipeline of a compiler to be readily deployed by the user. MLGO was among the first of such projects and it only strives to reduce the code size of a binary with an ML-based Inliner using Reinforcement Learning. This paper presents MLGOPerf; the first end-to-end framework capable of optimizing performance using LLVM's ML-Inliner. It employs a secondary ML model to generate rewards used for training a retargeted Reinforcement learning agent, previously used as the primary model by MLGO. It does so by predicting the post-inlining speedup of a function under analysis and it enables a fast training framework for the primary model which otherwise wouldn't be practical. The experimental results show MLGOPerf is able to gain up to 1.8% and 2.2% with respect to LLVM's optimization at O3 when trained for performance on SPEC CPU2006 and Cbench benchmarks, respectively. Furthermore, the proposed approach provides up to 26% increased opportunities to autotune code regions for our benchmarks which can be translated into an additional 3.7% speedup value.
summary: For the past 25 years, we have witnessed an extensive application of Machine Learning to the Compiler space; the selection and the phase-ordering problem. However, limited works have been upstreamed into the state-of-the-art compilers, i.e., LLVM, to seamlessly integrate the former into the optimization pipeline of a compiler to be readily deployed by the user. MLGO was among the first of such projects and it only strives to reduce the code size of a binary with an ML-based Inliner using Reinforcement Learning. This paper presents MLGOPerf; the first end-to-end framework capable of optimizing performance using LLVM's ML-Inliner. It employs a secondary ML model to generate rewards used for training a retargeted Reinforcement learning agent, previously used as the primary model by MLGO. It does so by predicting the post-inlining speedup of a function under analysis and it enables a fast training framework for the primary model which otherwise wouldn't be practical. The experimental results show MLGOPerf is able to gain up to 1.8% and 2.2% with respect to LLVM's optimization at O3 when trained for performance on SPEC CPU2006 and Cbench benchmarks, respectively. Furthermore, the proposed approach provides up to 26% increased opportunities to autotune code regions for our benchmarks which can be translated into an additional 3.7% speedup value.
abstract_zh: 在过去的 25 年里，我们见证了机器学习在编译器领域的广泛应用；选择和相序问题。然而，有限的工作已被上游到最先进的编译器（即 LLVM）中，以将前者无缝集成到编译器的优化管道中，以便用户轻松部署。 MLGO 是此类项目中的第一个，它只致力于通过使用强化学习的基于 ML 的内联器来减少二进制文件的代码大小。本文介绍了 MLGOPerf；第一个能够使用 LLVM 的 ML-Inliner 优化性能的端到端框架。它采用辅助 ML 模型来生成用于训练重新定位的强化学习代理的奖励，该奖励以前被 MLGO 用作主要模型。它通过预测所分析函数的内联后加速来实现这一点，并为主要模型提供快速训练框架，否则这是不切实际的。实验结果表明，在 SPEC CPU2006 和 Cbench 基准测试上进行性能训练时，MLGOPerf 相对于 LLVM 在 O3 的优化分别能够获得高达 1.8% 和 2.2% 的提升。此外，所提出的方法为我们的基准测试提供了高达 26% 的自动调整代码区域的机会，这可以转化为额外的 3.7% 加速值。

AI.tldr: 本文提出MLGOPerf，一个基于机器学习的内联优化框架，通过强化学习提升编译器性能优化效果。
AI.motivation: 尽管机器学习在编译器优化中已有应用，但现有工作（如MLGO）主要关注代码体积缩减，尚未有效集成到主流编译器（如LLVM）中实现性能优化。
AI.method: 构建二级机器学习模型预测内联后的性能提升，生成奖励信号训练强化学习代理，优化LLVM内联决策过程。

AI.conclusion: MLGOPerf通过机器学习引导的内联策略，有效提升了编译器性能优化能力，为未来智能编译技术提供了可行方案。

authors_text: Zan Gao,Hongwei Wei,Weili Guan,Jie Nie,Meng Wang,Shenyong Chen
title: A Semantic-aware Attention and Visual Shielding Network for Cloth-changing Person Re-identification
title_zh: 用于换衣服人员重新识别的语义感知注意力和视觉屏蔽网络
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2108.04527
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2108.04527
abstract: Cloth-changing person reidentification (ReID) is a newly emerging research topic that aims to retrieve pedestrians whose clothes are changed. Since the human appearance with different clothes exhibits large variations, it is very difficult for existing approaches to extract discriminative and robust feature representations. Current works mainly focus on body shape or contour sketches, but the human semantic information and the potential consistency of pedestrian features before and after changing clothes are not fully explored or are ignored. To solve these issues, in this work, a novel semantic-aware attention and visual shielding network for cloth-changing person ReID (abbreviated as SAVS) is proposed where the key idea is to shield clues related to the appearance of clothes and only focus on visual semantic information that is not sensitive to view/posture changes. Specifically, a visual semantic encoder is first employed to locate the human body and clothing regions based on human semantic segmentation information. Then, a human semantic attention module (HSA) is proposed to highlight the human semantic information and reweight the visual feature map. In addition, a visual clothes shielding module (VCS) is also designed to extract a more robust feature representation for the cloth-changing task by covering the clothing regions and focusing the model on the visual semantic information unrelated to the clothes. Most importantly, these two modules are jointly explored in an end-to-end unified framework. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method can significantly outperform state-of-the-art methods, and more robust features can be extracted for cloth-changing persons. Compared with FSAM (published in CVPR 2021), this method can achieve improvements of 32.7% (16.5%) and 14.9% (-) on the LTCC and PRCC datasets in terms of mAP (rank-1), respectively.

Since the human appearance with different clothes exhibits large variations, it is very difficult for existing approaches to extract discriminative and robust feature representations. In addition, a visual clothes shielding module (VCS) is also designed to extract a more robust feature representation for the cloth-changing task by covering the clothing regions and focusing the model on the visual semantic information unrelated to the clothes. Most importantly, these two modules are jointly explored in an end-to-end unified framework. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method can significantly outperform state-of-the-art methods, and more robust features can be extracted for cloth-changing persons. Compared with FSAM (published in CVPR 2021), this method can achieve improvements of 32.7% (16.5%) and 14.9% (-) on the LTCC and PRCC datasets in terms of mAP (rank-1), respectively.
abstract_zh: 换衣服的行人重新识别（ReID）是一个新兴的研究课题，旨在检索换衣服的行人。由于不同衣服的人体外观表现出很大的变化，现有的方法很难提取有区别的、鲁棒的特征表示。目前的工作主要集中在身体形状或轮廓草图上，但人类语义信息和换衣服前后行人特征的潜在一致性没有得到充分挖掘或被忽视。为了解决这些问题，在这项工作中，提出了一种新颖的语义感知注意力和视觉屏蔽网络，用于换衣服的人ReID（简称SAVS），其关键思想是屏蔽与衣服外观相关的线索，只关注对视图/姿势变化不敏感的视觉语义信息。具体来说，首先采用视觉语义编码器根据人体语义分割信息来定位人体和服装区域。然后，提出了人类语义注意模块（HSA）来突出人类语义信息并重新加权视觉特征图。此外，还设计了视觉衣服屏蔽模块（VCS），通过覆盖衣服区域并将模型聚焦于与衣服无关的视觉语义信息，为换衣服任务提取更鲁棒的特征表示。最重要的是，这两个模块是在端到端统一框架中共同探索的。大量实验表明，所提出的方法可以显着优于最先进的方法，并且可以为换衣服的人提取更鲁棒的特征。与 FSAM（发表于 CVPR 2021）相比，该方法在 LTCC 和 PRCC 数据集上的 mAP（rank-1）方面分别实现了 32.7%（16.5%）和 14.9%（-）的改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种语义感知注意力和视觉屏蔽网络（SAVS），用于解决衣物变化下的人再识别问题，通过提取与衣物无关的鲁棒视觉语义特征。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在衣物变化场景下难以提取稳定特征，主要依赖身体形状或轮廓信息，忽视了人体语义信息及衣着变化前后的特征一致性。
AI.method: 设计视觉语义编码器定位人体及衣物区域，结合人体语义注意力模块（HSA）强化语义信息，并通过视觉衣物屏蔽模块（VCS）遮蔽衣物区域，最终在统一框架中联合优化。
AI.result: 在LTCC和PRCC数据集上，相比FSAM方法，mAP指标分别提升32.7%和14.9%，rank-1指标提升16.5%。
AI.conclusion: SAVS通过分离衣物信息与语义特征，有效提升了衣物变化场景下的人再识别性能，验证了语义感知与视觉屏蔽策略的有效性。

authors_text: Xuejing Liu,Liang Li,Shuhui Wang,Zheng-Jun Zha,Zechao Li,Qi Tian,Qingming Huang
title: Entity-enhanced Adaptive Reconstruction Network for Weakly Supervised Referring Expression Grounding
title_zh: 用于弱监督引用表达基础的实体增强自适应重建网络
comment: 17 pages, 10 figures, accepted by TPAMI. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:1908.10568
comments: 17 pages, 10 figures, accepted by TPAMI. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:1908.10568
abstract: Weakly supervised Referring Expression Grounding (REG) aims to ground a particular target in an image described by a language expression while lacking the correspondence between target and expression. Two main problems exist in weakly supervised REG. First, the lack of region-level annotations introduces ambiguities between proposals and queries. Second, most previous weakly supervised REG methods ignore the discriminative location and context of the referent, causing difficulties in distinguishing the target from other same-category objects. To address the above challenges, we design an entity-enhanced adaptive reconstruction network (EARN). Specifically, EARN includes three modules: entity enhancement, adaptive grounding, and collaborative reconstruction. In entity enhancement, we calculate semantic similarity as supervision to select the candidate proposals. Adaptive grounding calculates the ranking score of candidate proposals upon subject, location and context with hierarchical attention. Collaborative reconstruction measures the ranking result from three perspectives: adaptive reconstruction, language reconstruction and attribute classification. The adaptive mechanism helps to alleviate the variance of different referring expressions. Experiments on five datasets show EARN outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods. Qualitative results demonstrate that the proposed EARN can better handle the situation where multiple objects of a particular category are situated together.
summary: Weakly supervised Referring Expression Grounding (REG) aims to ground a particular target in an image described by a language expression while lacking the correspondence between target and expression. Two main problems exist in weakly supervised REG. First, the lack of region-level annotations introduces ambiguities between proposals and queries. Second, most previous weakly supervised REG methods ignore the discriminative location and context of the referent, causing difficulties in distinguishing the target from other same-category objects. To address the above challenges, we design an entity-enhanced adaptive reconstruction network (EARN). Specifically, EARN includes three modules: entity enhancement, adaptive grounding, and collaborative reconstruction. In entity enhancement, we calculate semantic similarity as supervision to select the candidate proposals. Adaptive grounding calculates the ranking score of candidate proposals upon subject, location and context with hierarchical attention. Collaborative reconstruction measures the ranking result from three perspectives: adaptive reconstruction, language reconstruction and attribute classification. The adaptive mechanism helps to alleviate the variance of different referring expressions. Experiments on five datasets show EARN outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods. Qualitative results demonstrate that the proposed EARN can better handle the situation where multiple objects of a particular category are situated together.
abstract_zh: 弱监督引用表达基础（REG）旨在将特定目标置于由语言表达描述的图像中，但缺乏目标和表达之间的对应关系。弱监督 REG 存在两个主要问题。首先，缺乏区域级注释会导致提案和查询之间产生歧义。其次，大多数先前的弱监督 REG 方法忽略了指示对象的辨别位置和上下文，导致难以将目标与其他同类别对象区分开来。为了解决上述挑战，我们设计了一个实体增强自适应重建网络（EARN）。具体来说，EARN包括三个模块：实体增强、自适应接地和协作重建。在实体增强中，我们计算语义相似度作为监督来选择候选提案。自适应接地根据分层注意力的主题、位置和上下文计算候选提案的排名分数。协同重建从自适应重建、语言重建和属性分类三个角度衡量排名结果。自适应机制有助于减轻不同指称表达的差异。对五个数据集的实验表明 EARN 的性能优于现有的最先进方法。定性结果表明，所提出的 EARN 可以更好地处理特定类别的多个对象位于在一起的情况。

AI.tldr: 提出一种弱监督指代表达接地方法，解决缺乏区域注释和同类物体区分的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有弱监督REG方法因缺乏区域级标注导致候选框与查询歧义，且忽略目标位置与上下文信息，难以区分同类物体。
AI.method: 设计EARN网络，包含实体增强（语义相似度筛选候选）、自适应接地（分层注意力计算排名）和协作重建（多视角评估结果）三个模块。
AI.result: 在五个数据集上优于现有SOTA方法，定性结果验证其对同类物体聚集场景的处理能力。
AI.conclusion: EARN通过实体增强与多模块协作有效缓解弱监督REG的歧义问题，为复杂场景下的目标定位提供新思路。

authors_text: Kalin Stefanov,Bhawna Paliwal,Abhinav Dhall
title: Visual Representations of Physiological Signals for Fake Video Detection
title_zh: 用于虚假视频检测的生理信号的视觉表示
abstract: Realistic fake videos are a potential tool for spreading harmful misinformation given our increasing online presence and information intake. This paper presents a multimodal learning-based method for detection of real and fake videos. The method combines information from three modalities - audio, video, and physiology. We investigate two strategies for combining the video and physiology modalities, either by augmenting the video with information from the physiology or by novelly learning the fusion of those two modalities with a proposed Graph Convolutional Network architecture. Both strategies for combining the two modalities rely on a novel method for generation of visual representations of physiological signals. The detection of real and fake videos is then based on the dissimilarity between the audio and modified video modalities. The proposed method is evaluated on two benchmark datasets and the results show significant increase in detection performance compared to previous methods.
summary: Realistic fake videos are a potential tool for spreading harmful misinformation given our increasing online presence and information intake. This paper presents a multimodal learning-based method for detection of real and fake videos. The method combines information from three modalities - audio, video, and physiology. We investigate two strategies for combining the video and physiology modalities, either by augmenting the video with information from the physiology or by novelly learning the fusion of those two modalities with a proposed Graph Convolutional Network architecture. Both strategies for combining the two modalities rely on a novel method for generation of visual representations of physiological signals. The detection of real and fake videos is then based on the dissimilarity between the audio and modified video modalities. The proposed method is evaluated on two benchmark datasets and the results show significant increase in detection performance compared to previous methods.
abstract_zh: 鉴于我们不断增加的在线存在和信息摄入量，逼真的虚假视频是传播有害错误信息的潜在工具。本文提出了一种基于多模态学习的真假视频检测方法。该方法结合了来自三种模式的信息——音频、视频和生理学。我们研究了两种将视频和生理学模式相结合的策略，要么通过利用生理学信息来增强视频，要么通过新颖地学习这两种模式与所提出的图卷积网络架构的融合。结合两种模式的两种策略都依赖于一种生成生理信号视觉表示的新方法。然后，根据音频和修改后的视频模式之间的差异来检测真实和虚假视频。在两个基准数据集上对所提出的方法进行了评估，结果表明与以前的方法相比，检测性能显着提高。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于多模态学习的虚假视频检测方法，结合音频、视频和生理信号，通过生成生理信号的视觉表示并融合多模态信息提升检测性能。
AI.motivation: 随着网络信息传播的普及，高度逼真的虚假视频可能被用于传播有害虚假信息，亟需更有效的检测技术以应对这一威胁。
AI.method: 方法包含两种融合视频与生理信号的策略：一是将生理信息增强至视频中，二是通过图卷积网络（GCN）学习两者的融合表示，并基于音频与修改后视频模态的差异进行检测。
AI.result: 在两个基准数据集上的实验表明，该方法相比现有技术显著提升了虚假视频检测的准确率。
AI.conclusion: 通过创新性地整合生理信号的视觉表示与多模态融合策略，本文为虚假视频检测提供了更有效的技术方案。

authors_text: Guoqing Liu,Mengzhang Cai,Li Zhao,Tao Qin,Adrian Brown,Jimmy Bischoff,Tie-Yan Liu
title: Inspector: Pixel-Based Automated Game Testing via Exploration, Detection, and Investigation
title_zh: Inspector：通过探索、检测和调查进行基于像素的自动化游戏测试
comment: Accepted as IEEE CoG2022 proceedings paper (Oral)
comments: Accepted as IEEE CoG2022 proceedings paper (Oral)
abstract: Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) has attracted much attention in automated game testing. Early attempts rely on game internal information for game space exploration, thus requiring deep integration with games, which is inconvenient for practical applications. In this work, we propose using only screenshots/pixels as input for automated game testing and build a general game testing agent, Inspector, that can be easily applied to different games without deep integration with games. In addition to covering all game space for testing, our agent tries to take human-like behaviors to interact with key objects in a game, since some bugs usually happen in player-object interactions. Inspector is based on purely pixel inputs and comprises three key modules: game space explorer, key object detector, and human-like object investigator. Game space explorer aims to explore the whole game space by using a curiosity-based reward function with pixel inputs. Key object detector aims to detect key objects in a game, based on a small number of labeled screenshots. Human-like object investigator aims to mimic human behaviors for investigating key objects via imitation learning. We conduct experiments on two popular video games: Shooter Game and Action RPG Game. Experiment results demonstrate the effectiveness of Inspector in exploring game space, detecting key objects, and investigating objects. Moreover, Inspector successfully discovers two potential bugs in those two games. The demo video of Inspector is available at https://github.com/Inspector-GameTesting/Inspector-GameTesting.
summary: Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) has attracted much attention in automated game testing. Early attempts rely on game internal information for game space exploration, thus requiring deep integration with games, which is inconvenient for practical applications. In this work, we propose using only screenshots/pixels as input for automated game testing and build a general game testing agent, Inspector, that can be easily applied to different games without deep integration with games. In addition to covering all game space for testing, our agent tries to take human-like behaviors to interact with key objects in a game, since some bugs usually happen in player-object interactions. Inspector is based on purely pixel inputs and comprises three key modules: game space explorer, key object detector, and human-like object investigator. Game space explorer aims to explore the whole game space by using a curiosity-based reward function with pixel inputs. Key object detector aims to detect key objects in a game, based on a small number of labeled screenshots. Human-like object investigator aims to mimic human behaviors for investigating key objects via imitation learning. We conduct experiments on two popular video games: Shooter Game and Action RPG Game. Experiment results demonstrate the effectiveness of Inspector in exploring game space, detecting key objects, and investigating objects. Moreover, Inspector successfully discovers two potential bugs in those two games. The demo video of Inspector is available at https://github.com/Inspector-GameTesting/Inspector-GameTesting.
abstract_zh: 深度强化学习（DRL）在自动化游戏测试中引起了广泛关注。早期的尝试依赖游戏内部信息进行游戏空间探索，因此需要与游戏深度结合，不方便实际应用。在这项工作中，我们建议仅使用屏幕截图/像素作为自动化游戏测试的输入，并构建一个通用的游戏测试代理 Inspector，它可以轻松应用于不同的游戏，而无需与游戏深度集成。除了覆盖所有游戏空间进行测试之外，我们的代理还尝试采用类似人类的行为来与游戏中的关键对象进行交互，因为一些错误通常发生在玩家与对象的交互中。 Inspector 基于纯粹的像素输入，包含三个关键模块：游戏空间探索器、关键对象检测器和类人对象调查器。游戏空间探索器旨在通过使用基于好奇心的奖励函数和像素输入来探索整个游戏空间。关键对象检测器旨在根据少量标记的屏幕截图来检测游戏中的关键对象。类人物体调查员旨在通过模仿学习来模仿人类行为来调查关键物体。我们对两种流行的视频游戏进行了实验：射击游戏和动作角色扮演游戏。实验结果证明了 Inspector 在探索游戏空间、检测关键对象和调查对象方面的有效性。此外，Inspector 成功发现了这两款游戏中的两个潜在错误。 Inspector 的演示视频可在 https://github.com/Inspector-GameTesting/Inspector-GameTesting 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Inspector，一种基于像素输入的通用游戏自动化测试代理，通过探索、检测和调查模块实现跨游戏的自动化测试。
AI.motivation: 传统深度强化学习依赖游戏内部信息进行测试，需深度集成游戏代码，限制了实用性。本文旨在开发无需深度集成、仅依赖像素输入的通用测试方法，并通过类人行为发现交互相关漏洞。
AI.method: Inspector包含三个模块：基于好奇心奖励的探索器覆盖游戏空间；基于少量标注截图的关键物体检测器；以及通过模仿学习实现的类人交互调查器，整体仅依赖像素输入。
AI.result: 在两款流行游戏中验证了Inspector的有效性，成功探索游戏空间、检测关键物体并发现两个潜在漏洞。
AI.conclusion: Inspector证明了纯像素输入的自动化游戏测试可行性，为跨游戏通用测试提供了实用方案。

search_text: Inspector: Pixel-Based Automated Game Testing via Exploration, Detection, and Investigation Inspector：通过探索、检测和调查进行基于像素的自动化游戏测试 Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) has attracted much attention in automated game testing. Early attempts rely on game internal information for game space exploration, thus requiring deep integration with games, which is inconvenient for practical applications. In this work, we propose using only screenshots/pixels as input for automated game testing and build a general game testing agent, Inspector, that can be easily applied to different games without deep integration with games. In addition to covering all game space for testing, our agent tries to take human-like behaviors to interact with key objects in a game, since some bugs usually happen in player-object interactions. Inspector is based on purely pixel inputs and comprises three key modules: game space explorer, key object detector, and human-like object investigator. Game space explorer aims to explore the whole game space by using a curiosity-based reward function with pixel inputs. Key object detector aims to detect key objects in a game, based on a small number of labeled screenshots. Human-like object investigator aims to mimic human behaviors for investigating key objects via imitation learning. We conduct experiments on two popular video games: Shooter Game and Action RPG Game. Experiment results demonstrate the effectiveness of Inspector in exploring game space, detecting key objects, and investigating objects. Moreover, Inspector successfully discovers two potential bugs in those two games. The demo video of Inspector is available at https://github.com/Inspector-GameTesting/Inspector-GameTesting. 深度强化学习（DRL）在自动化游戏测试中引起了广泛关注。早期的尝试依赖游戏内部信息进行游戏空间探索，因此需要与游戏深度结合，不方便实际应用。在这项工作中，我们建议仅使用屏幕截图/像素作为自动化游戏测试的输入，并构建一个通用的游戏测试代理 Inspector，它可以轻松应用于不同的游戏，而无需与游戏深度集成。除了覆盖所有游戏空间进行测试之外，我们的代理还尝试采用类似人类的行为来与游戏中的关键对象进行交互，因为一些错误通常发生在玩家与对象的交互中。 Inspector 基于纯粹的像素输入，包含三个关键模块：游戏空间探索器、关键对象检测器和类人对象调查器。游戏空间探索器旨在通过使用基于好奇心的奖励函数和像素输入来探索整个游戏空间。关键对象检测器旨在根据少量标记的屏幕截图来检测游戏中的关键对象。类人物体调查员旨在通过模仿学习来模仿人类行为来调查关键物体。我们对两种流行的视频游戏进行了实验：射击游戏和动作角色扮演游戏。实验结果证明了 Inspector 在探索游戏空间、检测关键对象和调查对象方面的有效性。此外，Inspector 成功发现了这两款游戏中的两个潜在错误。 Inspector 的演示视频可在 https://github.com/Inspector-GameTesting/Inspector-GameTesting 获取。 本文提出Inspector，一种基于像素输入的通用游戏自动化测试代理，通过探索、检测和调查模块实现跨游戏的自动化测试。 Guoqing Liu,Mengzhang Cai,Li Zhao,Tao Qin,Adrian Brown,Jimmy Bischoff,Tie-Yan Liu cs.AI

authors_text: Xuelong Li,Ziheng Jiao,Hongyuan Zhang,Rui Zhang
title: Deep Manifold Learning with Graph Mining
title_zh: 深度流形学习与图挖掘
abstract: Admittedly, Graph Convolution Network (GCN) has achieved excellent results on graph datasets such as social networks, citation networks, etc. However, softmax used as the decision layer in these frameworks is generally optimized with thousands of iterations via gradient descent. Furthermore, due to ignoring the inner distribution of the graph nodes, the decision layer might lead to an unsatisfactory performance in semi-supervised learning with less label support. To address the referred issues, we propose a novel graph deep model with a non-gradient decision layer for graph mining. Firstly, manifold learning is unified with label local-structure preservation to capture the topological information of the nodes. Moreover, owing to the non-gradient property, closed-form solutions is achieved to be employed as the decision layer for GCN. Particularly, a joint optimization method is designed for this graph model, which extremely accelerates the convergence of the model. Finally, extensive experiments show that the proposed model has achieved state-of-the-art performance compared to the current models.
summary: Admittedly, Graph Convolution Network (GCN) has achieved excellent results on graph datasets such as social networks, citation networks, etc. However, softmax used as the decision layer in these frameworks is generally optimized with thousands of iterations via gradient descent. Furthermore, due to ignoring the inner distribution of the graph nodes, the decision layer might lead to an unsatisfactory performance in semi-supervised learning with less label support. To address the referred issues, we propose a novel graph deep model with a non-gradient decision layer for graph mining. Firstly, manifold learning is unified with label local-structure preservation to capture the topological information of the nodes. Moreover, owing to the non-gradient property, closed-form solutions is achieved to be employed as the decision layer for GCN. Particularly, a joint optimization method is designed for this graph model, which extremely accelerates the convergence of the model. Finally, extensive experiments show that the proposed model has achieved state-of-the-art performance compared to the current models.
abstract_zh: 诚然，图卷积网络（GCN）在社交网络、引文网络等图数据集上取得了优异的结果。然而，这些框架中用作决策层的softmax通常通过梯度下降进行数千次迭代优化。此外，由于忽略了图节点的内部分布，决策层可能会导致标签支持较少的半监督学习性能不理想。为了解决所提到的问题，我们提出了一种新颖的图深度模型，具有用于图挖掘的非梯度决策层。首先，流形学习与标签局部结构保存相结合，捕获节点的拓扑信息。此外，由于非梯度特性，实现了封闭式解决方案，可用作 GCN 的决策层。特别是，针对该图模型设计了联合优化方法，极大地加速了模型的收敛。最后，大量的实验表明，与当前模型相比，所提出的模型已经实现了最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 提出一种新型图深度学习模型，解决现有GCN在半监督学习中的性能问题
AI.motivation: 现有GCN的softmax决策层依赖梯度下降优化，计算成本高且在标签稀缺时表现不佳
AI.method: 结合流形学习与标签局部结构保持，设计非梯度决策层的闭式解，并采用联合优化加速收敛
AI.result: 在多个基准数据集上达到最先进性能，收敛速度显著提升
AI.conclusion: 提出的模型在保持高精度的同时提升计算效率，为图挖掘提供有效解决方案

authors_text: Cagri Arisoy,Anuradha Mandal,Nitesh Saxena
title: Human Brains Can't Detect Fake News: A Neuro-Cognitive Study of Textual Disinformation Susceptibility
title_zh: 人脑无法检测假新闻：文本虚假信息易感性的神经认知研究
comment: 12 pages, 9 tables, 2 figures, published in PST2022
comments: 12 pages, 9 tables, 2 figures, published in PST2022
abstract: The spread of digital disinformation (aka "fake news") is arguably one of the most significant threats on the Internet which can cause individual and societal harm of large scales. The susceptibility to fake news attacks hinges on whether Internet users perceive a fake news article/snippet to be legitimate after reading it. In this paper, we attempt to garner an in-depth understanding of users' susceptibility to text-centric fake news attacks via a neuro-cognitive methodology. We investigate the neural underpinnings relevant to fake/real news through EEG. We run an experiment with human users to pursue a thorough investigation of users' perception and cognitive processing of fake/real news. We analyze the neural activity associated with the fake/real news detection task for different categories of news articles. Our results show there may be no statistically significant or automatically inferable differences in the way the human brain processes the fake vs. real news, while marked differences are observed when people are subject to (real/fake) news vs. resting state and even between some different categories of fake news. This neuro-cognitive finding may help to justify users' susceptibility to fake news attacks, as also confirmed from the behavioral analysis. In other words, the fake news articles may seem almost indistinguishable from the real news articles in both behavioral and neural domains. Our work serves to dissect the fundamental neural phenomena underlying fake news attacks and explains users' susceptibility to these attacks through the limits of human biology. We believe this could be a notable insight for the researchers and practitioners suggesting the human detection of fake news might be ineffective, which may also have an adverse impact on the design of automated detection approaches that crucially rely upon human labeling of text articles for building training models
summary: The spread of digital disinformation (aka "fake news") is arguably one of the most significant threats on the Internet which can cause individual and societal harm of large scales. The susceptibility to fake news attacks hinges on whether Internet users perceive a fake news article/snippet to be legitimate after reading it. In this paper, we attempt to garner an in-depth understanding of users' susceptibility to text-centric fake news attacks via a neuro-cognitive methodology. We investigate the neural underpinnings relevant to fake/real news through EEG. We analyze the neural activity associated with the fake/real news detection task for different categories of news articles. Our results show there may be no statistically significant or automatically inferable differences in the way the human brain processes the fake vs. real news, while marked differences are observed when people are subject to (real/fake) news vs. resting state and even between some different categories of fake news. This neuro-cognitive finding may help to justify users' susceptibility to fake news attacks, as also confirmed from the behavioral analysis. In other words, the fake news articles may seem almost indistinguishable from the real news articles in both behavioral and neural domains. Our work serves to dissect the fundamental neural phenomena underlying fake news attacks and explains users' susceptibility to these attacks through the limits of human biology. We believe this could be a notable insight for the researchers and practitioners suggesting the human detection of fake news might be ineffective, which may also have an adverse impact on the design of automated detection approaches that crucially rely upon human labeling of text articles for building training models
abstract_zh: 数字虚假信息（又名“假新闻”）的传播可以说是互联网上最重大的威胁之一，可能造成大规模的个人和社会伤害。假新闻攻击的敏感性取决于互联网用户在阅读假新闻文章/片段后是否认为其合法。在本文中，我们试图通过神经认知方法深入了解用户对以文本为中心的假新闻攻击的敏感性。我们通过脑电图研究与假/真实新闻相关的神经基础。我们对人类用户进行了一项实验，以彻底调查用户对假/真实新闻的感知和认知处理。我们分析了与不同类别的新闻文章的假/真实新闻检测任务相关的神经活动。我们的研究结果表明，人脑处理假新闻与真实新闻的方式可能没有统计学上的显着差异或自动推断的差异，而当人们受到（真/假）新闻与静息状态的影响时，甚至在某些不同类别的假新闻之间，就会观察到显着的差异。这一神经认知发现可能有助于证明用户对假新闻攻击的易感性，行为分析也证实了这一点。换句话说，假新闻文章在行为和神经领域似乎与真实新闻文章几乎没有区别。我们的工作旨在剖析假新闻攻击背后的基本神经现象，并通过人类生物学的限制解释用户对这些攻击的敏感性。我们认为，这对于研究人员和从业者来说可能是一个值得注意的见解，表明人工检测假新闻可能无效，这也可能对自动检测方法的设计产生不利影响，这些方法主要依赖于文本文章的人工标记来构建训练模型

AI.tldr: 该论文通过神经认知方法研究人类对文本虚假新闻的易感性，利用脑电图（EEG）分析大脑处理虚假与真实新闻的神经机制。
AI.motivation: 数字虚假新闻的传播对个人和社会构成重大威胁，理解用户易受攻击的原因对于设计有效的检测方法至关重要。
AI.method: 通过EEG实验记录人类阅读虚假/真实新闻时的脑电活动，分析不同类别新闻与休息状态下的神经差异。
AI.result: 实验发现大脑处理虚假与真实新闻的神经活动无显著差异，但新闻内容与休息状态或不同虚假新闻类别间存在明显差异。
AI.conclusion: 人类生物局限性导致无法有效区分虚假新闻，这一发现对依赖人工标注训练模型的自动化检测方法设计具有重要启示。

result: 实验发现大脑处理虚假与真实新闻的神经活动无显著差异，但新闻内容与休息状态或不同虚假新闻类别间存在明显差异。
conclusion: 人类生物局限性导致无法有效区分虚假新闻，这一发现对依赖人工标注训练模型的自动化检测方法设计具有重要启示。
search_text: Human Brains Can't Detect Fake News: A Neuro-Cognitive Study of Textual Disinformation Susceptibility 人脑无法检测假新闻：文本虚假信息易感性的神经认知研究 The spread of digital disinformation (aka "fake news") is arguably one of the most significant threats on the Internet which can cause individual and societal harm of large scales. The susceptibility to fake news attacks hinges on whether Internet users perceive a fake news article/snippet to be legitimate after reading it. In this paper, we attempt to garner an in-depth understanding of users' susceptibility to text-centric fake news attacks via a neuro-cognitive methodology. We investigate the neural underpinnings relevant to fake/real news through EEG. We run an experiment with human users to pursue a thorough investigation of users' perception and cognitive processing of fake/real news. We analyze the neural activity associated with the fake/real news detection task for different categories of news articles. Our results show there may be no statistically significant or automatically inferable differences in the way the human brain processes the fake vs. real news, while marked differences are observed when people are subject to (real/fake) news vs. resting state and even between some different categories of fake news. This neuro-cognitive finding may help to justify users' susceptibility to fake news attacks, as also confirmed from the behavioral analysis. In other words, the fake news articles may seem almost indistinguishable from the real news articles in both behavioral and neural domains. Our work serves to dissect the fundamental neural phenomena underlying fake news attacks and explains users' susceptibility to these attacks through the limits of human biology. We believe this could be a notable insight for the researchers and practitioners suggesting the human detection of fake news might be ineffective, which may also have an adverse impact on the design of automated detection approaches that crucially rely upon human labeling of text articles for building training models 数字虚假信息（又名“假新闻”）的传播可以说是互联网上最重大的威胁之一，可能造成大规模的个人和社会伤害。假新闻攻击的敏感性取决于互联网用户在阅读假新闻文章/片段后是否认为其合法。在本文中，我们试图通过神经认知方法深入了解用户对以文本为中心的假新闻攻击的敏感性。我们通过脑电图研究与假/真实新闻相关的神经基础。我们对人类用户进行了一项实验，以彻底调查用户对假/真实新闻的感知和认知处理。我们分析了与不同类别的新闻文章的假/真实新闻检测任务相关的神经活动。我们的研究结果表明，人脑处理假新闻与真实新闻的方式可能没有统计学上的显着差异或自动推断的差异，而当人们受到（真/假）新闻与静息状态的影响时，甚至在某些不同类别的假新闻之间，就会观察到显着的差异。这一神经认知发现可能有助于证明用户对假新闻攻击的易感性，行为分析也证实了这一点。换句话说，假新闻文章在行为和神经领域似乎与真实新闻文章几乎没有区别。我们的工作旨在剖析假新闻攻击背后的基本神经现象，并通过人类生物学的限制解释用户对这些攻击的敏感性。我们认为，这对于研究人员和从业者来说可能是一个值得注意的见解，表明人工检测假新闻可能无效，这也可能对自动检测方法的设计产生不利影响，这些方法主要依赖于文本文章的人工标记来构建训练模型 该论文通过神经认知方法研究人类对文本虚假新闻的易感性，利用脑电图（EEG）分析大脑处理虚假与真实新闻的神经机制。 Cagri Arisoy,Anuradha Mandal,Nitesh Saxena cs.CL,cs.CR,cs.CY,cs.HC,cs.SI

authors_text: Qiying Yu,Jieming Lou,Xianyuan Zhan,Qizhang Li,Wangmeng Zuo,Yang Liu,Jingjing Liu
title: Adversarial Contrastive Learning via Asymmetric InfoNCE
title_zh: 通过不对称 InfoNCE 进行对抗性对比学习
comment: Accepted by ECCV 2022. Code is available at https://github.com/yqy2001/A-InfoNCE
comments: Accepted by ECCV 2022. Code is available at https://github.com/yqy2001/A-InfoNCE
abstract: Contrastive learning (CL) has recently been applied to adversarial learning tasks. Such practice considers adversarial samples as additional positive views of an instance, and by maximizing their agreements with each other, yields better adversarial robustness. However, this mechanism can be potentially flawed, since adversarial perturbations may cause instance-level identity confusion, which can impede CL performance by pulling together different instances with separate identities. To address this issue, we propose to treat adversarial samples unequally when contrasted, with an asymmetric InfoNCE objective ($A-InfoNCE$) that allows discriminating considerations of adversarial samples. Specifically, adversaries are viewed as inferior positives that induce weaker learning signals, or as hard negatives exhibiting higher contrast to other negative samples. In the asymmetric fashion, the adverse impacts of conflicting objectives between CL and adversarial learning can be effectively mitigated. Experiments show that our approach consistently outperforms existing Adversarial CL methods across different finetuning schemes without additional computational cost. The proposed A-InfoNCE is also a generic form that can be readily extended to other CL methods. Code is available at https://github.com/yqy2001/A-InfoNCE.
summary: Contrastive learning (CL) has recently been applied to adversarial learning tasks. Such practice considers adversarial samples as additional positive views of an instance, and by maximizing their agreements with each other, yields better adversarial robustness. However, this mechanism can be potentially flawed, since adversarial perturbations may cause instance-level identity confusion, which can impede CL performance by pulling together different instances with separate identities. To address this issue, we propose to treat adversarial samples unequally when contrasted, with an asymmetric InfoNCE objective ($A-InfoNCE$) that allows discriminating considerations of adversarial samples. Specifically, adversaries are viewed as inferior positives that induce weaker learning signals, or as hard negatives exhibiting higher contrast to other negative samples. In the asymmetric fashion, the adverse impacts of conflicting objectives between CL and adversarial learning can be effectively mitigated. Experiments show that our approach consistently outperforms existing Adversarial CL methods across different finetuning schemes without additional computational cost. The proposed A-InfoNCE is also a generic form that can be readily extended to other CL methods. Code is available at https://github.com/yqy2001/A-InfoNCE.
abstract_zh: 对比学习（CL）最近已应用于对抗性学习任务。这种做法将对抗性样本视为实例的额外积极观点，并通过最大化它们之间的一致性，产生更好的对抗性鲁棒性。然而，这种机制可能存在潜在缺陷，因为对抗性扰动可能会导致实例级身份混淆，这可能会通过将具有不同身份的不同实例放在一起来阻碍 CL 性能。为了解决这个问题，我们建议在对比时不平等地对待对抗性样本，使用不对称的 InfoNCE 目标 ($A-InfoNCE$)，允许对对抗性样本进行区分考虑。具体来说，对手被视为导致学习信号较弱的劣质阳性样本，或者被视为与其他阴性样本表现出更高对比度的硬阴性样本。以不对称的方式，可以有效减轻 CL 和对抗性学习之间目标冲突的不利影响。实验表明，我们的方法在不同的微调方案中始终优于现有的对抗 CL 方法，而无需额外的计算成本。所提出的 A-InfoNCE 也是一种通用形式，可以轻松扩展到其他 CL 方法。代码可在 https://github.com/yqy2001/A-InfoNCE 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种不对称的InfoNCE目标（A-InfoNCE），通过区分对抗样本在对比学习中的作用，提升对抗鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有对抗对比学习方法将对抗样本视为正样本，但对抗扰动可能导致实例身份混淆，损害对比学习性能。
AI.method: 设计A-InfoNCE目标，将对抗样本视为弱正样本或强负样本，通过不对称对比机制缓解对抗学习与对比学习的冲突。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在不同微调方案下均优于现有对抗对比学习方法，且无需额外计算成本，具有通用性。
AI.conclusion: A-InfoNCE通过区分对抗样本的作用，有效提升了对抗鲁棒性，为对比学习提供了通用解决方案。

search_text: Adversarial Contrastive Learning via Asymmetric InfoNCE 通过不对称 InfoNCE 进行对抗性对比学习 Contrastive learning (CL) has recently been applied to adversarial learning tasks. Such practice considers adversarial samples as additional positive views of an instance, and by maximizing their agreements with each other, yields better adversarial robustness. However, this mechanism can be potentially flawed, since adversarial perturbations may cause instance-level identity confusion, which can impede CL performance by pulling together different instances with separate identities. To address this issue, we propose to treat adversarial samples unequally when contrasted, with an asymmetric InfoNCE objective ($A-InfoNCE$) that allows discriminating considerations of adversarial samples. Specifically, adversaries are viewed as inferior positives that induce weaker learning signals, or as hard negatives exhibiting higher contrast to other negative samples. In the asymmetric fashion, the adverse impacts of conflicting objectives between CL and adversarial learning can be effectively mitigated. Experiments show that our approach consistently outperforms existing Adversarial CL methods across different finetuning schemes without additional computational cost. The proposed A-InfoNCE is also a generic form that can be readily extended to other CL methods.
